testcase.name: Data-driven Demo Web Shop workflow with login, preconditions, and postconditions

failure.message: demo-webshop-data-driven-login.spec.ts:143:5 Data-driven Demo Web Shop workflow with login, preconditions, and postconditions

testcase.name: Dynamic Properties - verify enablement, visibility, and color change
testcase.system-out: Step 1: Open DemoQA homepage Step 2: Open Elements section Step 3: Open Dynamic Properties page Step 4: Verify initial state Initial colorChange class: mt-4 btn btn-primary Step 5: Wait until the disabled button becomes enabled Step 6: Wait until the hidden button becomes visible Step 7: Wait until the color change button style/class changes Updated colorChange class: mt-4 text-danger btn btn-primary Test completed successfully

testcase.name: Progress Bar start, stop, complete, and reset
testcase.system-out: Step 1: Open DemoQA homepage Step 2: Open Widgets section Step 3: Open Progress Bar page Step 4: Verify initial state Step 5: Start progress Step 6: Wait until progress reaches at least 40% Step 7: Stop progress Stopped at: 49% Step 8: Resume progress Step 9: Wait until progress reaches 100% Step 10: Verify Reset button is visible Step 11: Reset progress Step 12: Verify progress returns to 0%

testcase.name: Create second page in Web Tables, delete record from page 2, and verify pagination returns to page 1
testcase.system-out: Step 1: Open DemoQA homepage Step 2: Open Elements section Step 3: Open Web Tables page Step 4: Read current table state Initial populated rows: 3 Rows per page: 10 Records needed to create page 2: 8 Step 5: Add enough records to create a second page Adding record: auto.user1@example.com Adding record: auto.user2@example.com Adding record: auto.user3@example.com Adding record: auto.user4@example.com Adding record: auto.user5@example.com Adding record: auto.user6@example.com Adding record: auto.user7@example.com Adding record: auto.user8@example.com Step 6: Navigate to page 2 Step 7: Delete record on page 2: auto.user8@example.com Step 8: Verify pagination returns to page 1 and total pages becomes 1 Test completed successfully

testcase.name: Add multiple products above 900 USD to cart, update quantity, and verify subtotal
testcase.system-out: Step 1: Open homepage Precondition: cleaning shopping cart Step 2: Collect candidate products from category pages Scanning category: https://demowebshop.tricentis.com/notebooks Candidate found: 14.1-inch Laptop | price: 1590 Scanning category: https://demowebshop.tricentis.com/desktops Candidate found: Build your own computer | price: 1200 Candidate found: Build your own expensive computer | price: 1800 Candidate found: Elite Desktop PC | price: 1350 Step 3: Add two qualifying products to cart Opening product page: 14.1-inch Laptop Notification for 14.1-inch Laptop: The product has been added to your shopping cart Added successfully: 14.1-inch Laptop Opening product page: Build your own computer Notification for Build your own computer: The product has been added to your shopping cart Added successfully: Build your own computer Added products: - 14.1-inch Laptop | 1590 - Build your own computer | 1200 Step 4: Open shopping cart Step 5: Verify both selected products are present in cart Step 6: Update quantity of the first product from 1 to 2 Step 7: Verify arithmetic for each cart row Row 1: unit=1590, qty=2, subtotal=3180 Row 2: unit=1250, qty=1, subtotal=1250 Calculated cart total from row subtotals: 4430 Step 8: Clean cart after test Test completed successfully

testcase.name: Application home page should be opened
